name: Snow Report 2026-03-23 16:15:34
report:
Good afternoon, Pico family, 

It was a slick one out there today, as an overnight freeze didn’t do us any favors with getting more than a select number of trails open. The next couple of days look friendly for maintaining our snowpack, so we’ll be adding a number of trails back to our count ahead of Thursday. 

Conditions today remained as a mix of loose granular with spots of hardpack on steeper surfaces. Another round of grooming before Thursday morning will bring many of our surfaces back to life as we approach our final few days of the season. 

As a friendly reminder, we’ll be closed for our usual midweek break before returning for action on Thursday, March 26th. Our Pico Parks in Prospector and Panhandler will also be open once again for some softer spring laps. 

Keep an eye on our events calendar ahead of our last events of this season! The Vertical Challenge returns for a day of free, all-ages racing this Saturday, and our annual Season Pass Holder Appreciation Party will take place this upcoming Sunday. 

Lastly, don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

We’ll see you all again soon! 

- Cullen 

  

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. 

Uphill Travel       

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - CLOSED
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – CLOSED     

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.                  

Mountain Services       

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.      

Tickets & Lessons       

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online.
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation       

Pico Fitness Center                                  

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.          

Hours of Operation:       

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.              

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-23 05:51:03
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

We made it through another Sunday with a little bit of everything falling from the sky and some softer surfaces mixed in. Today will feel a bit more like winter than spring once again, with surfaces hardening up a bit overnight.

The weather today stays mostly cloudy with a few snow showers potentially drifting through. Temperatures will hover in the mid-20s during the day with a gentle north breeze.

Conditions yesterday started out with a touch of scattered hardpack, but as temperatures eased up, the snow became more edge-able and fun to ski & ride. Runs like Expressway and Fools Gold offered some enjoyable turns out there. Today, surfaces are expected to firm back up, so dialing in those edges will go a long way. 

Our grooming team went after Forty Niner, Fools Gold, Gold Rush, Expressway, and more. We had to carry out an adjusted grooming plan last night, with all of our groomed terrain getting smoothed out early this AM to prepare surfaces the best we can. Trails that receive a groom will be the go-to today. Check out our full list of groomed terrain on our Lifts & Trails Report. 

Just a heads up: due to our adjusted grooming plan, our Pico Parks in Prospector and Panhandler will not open today. We'll have them back online for you on Thursday.

The Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, Golden Express, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled for a 9:00 a.m. opening this morning.

Lastly, don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Enjoy your week!

- Cullen

name: Snow Report 2026-03-22 16:00:02
report:
Good evening, Pico family, 

We made it through another Sunday with a little bit of everything falling from the sky and some softer surfaces mixed in. Conditions started out with a touch of scattered hardpack, but as temperatures eased up, the snow became more edge-able and fun to ski & ride.

Expect a bit of everything overnight with rain, sleet, and some freezing rain in the mix. Tomorrow stays mostly cloudy with a few snow showers drifting through, but nothing major, as temps hover in the mid-20s with a steady north breeze.

Runs like Expressway and Fools Gold offered some enjoyable turns, and overall conditions were a nice step up from yesterday. Looking ahead to tomorrow, surfaces are expected to firm back up, so dialing in those edges will go a long way. 

Our grooming team is after Forty Niner, Fools Gold, Gold Rush, Expressway, and more. Trails that receive a groom will be the go-to tomorrow as we expect surfaces to harden as temps drop overnight. Check out our full list of groomed terrain on our Lifts & Trails Report to find your best options for skiing & riding this weekend. 

The Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, Golden Express, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled for a 9:00 a.m. opening.

Lastly, don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Have a great week!

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-22 06:00:26
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

We were dealt some firmer surfaces out there yesterday, but once that sun showed up it turned into a pretty sweet day for cruising. We didn't get any snow last night, so we'll have to see what this evening has in store. Today brings a high of 37 at the base and 34 near the peak, with strong winds from the south around 30 to 40 mph. 

Surfaces have been on the firmer side recently for skiing and riding and hopefully thing will slightly soften up today. Even if snowfall stays light, temperatures are expected to climb above freezing by midday. If those warmer temps do not come through, we may see a bit of snow instead. Either way, our operations team will be hard at work to keep things skiing and riding well.

Our grooming got after the same plan as last night. Head over to Ace of Clubs or up to Forty Niner for some resurfaced cruising and check out our full list of groomed terrain on our Lifts & Trails Report to find your best options for skiing & riding this weekend. 

The Bonanza Carpet, Little Pico Triple, Golden Express, and Summit Express are all scheduled for an 8:30 a.m. opening, with Knomes Knoll following suit at 9:00 a.m. 

Lastly, don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Get out there and enjoy the rest of your weekend! 

- Evan

With 0 to 8 inches of snow on the forecast, I think we’ll just have to wait and see on this one. Tomorrow brings a high of 37 at the base and 34 near the peak, with strong winds from the south around 30 to 40 mph. 

Surfaces were on the firmer side for skiing and riding today, but we could see a nice change over the next 24 hours. Even if snowfall stays light, temperatures are expected to climb above freezing by midday, which should help soften things up. name: Snow Report 2026-03-21 06:00:34
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

We added on another 2” of snow yesterday before the less favorable form of precipitation started up. That wraped up later in the evening, though, and our groomers worked their magic to keep our surfaces friendly for the morning. 

Temperatures will drop a bit leading into the morning, floating right around freezing at our base throughout the day. Winds will pick up slightly early on before calming down a bit in the afternoon. We’re not due for more precipitation until later in the evening, when we're again due for a snowy mix heading into Sunday. 

Conditions were soft and turns were easy out there yesterday. Snow surfaces should harden up a bit heading into today, but it should be just enough to stay nice and carvable. K.A. continues to offer a fun route down from the summit, and we again groomed that trail overnight. Check out our full list of groomed terrain on our Lifts & Trails Report to find your best options for skiing & riding this weekend. Expressway and Fools Gold have been the place to be for wide open cruising as of late. 

The Bonanza Carpet, Little Pico Triple, Golden Express, and Summit Express are all scheduled for an 8:30 a.m. opening, with Knomes Knoll following suit at 9:00 a.m. 

Lastly, don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Get out there and enjoy the rest of spring! 

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-20 05:59:18
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

Flurries fell sporadically throughout the day yesterday giving us a wintry feel and an inch of snow by late afternoon. Thanks to the efforts of our grooming team, surfaces continue to get better, and conditions will likely continue to improve into the early morning. 

Snow rolls in today, turning heavier by late afternoon with a mix of snow, sleet, and rain as temps climb into the mid-30s. Expect breezy conditions and about 1–2 inches of accumulation before things transition.

Surfaces continue to get better and a slight warm up coupled with another night of grooming should leave us in decent shape. Our grooming team is after the same plan with Upper K.A. as an addition. Other trails that saw a resurface include Gold Rush, Swinger, and 14 other trails. For the full rundown of groomed terrain, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, Knomes Knoll Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all slated to open at 9:00 a.m. to get you where you want to go.

As a reminder, the New England Women's Demo will be taking place in our base area all day today, with some après activity in the Last Run Lounge at the end of the day. Feel free to join the raffle, with all proceeds going to benefit Boarding 4 Breast Cancer!

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Have a fun Friday! 

 – Evan

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.     

Uphill Travel     

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - CLOSED
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open    

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.                

Mountain Services     

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.

  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation

Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-19 16:00:08
report:
Good evening, Pico family,

Flurries fell throughout the day providing a wintry feel here in mid-March. Thanks to the efforts of our grooming team, surfaces made an improvement, and conditions will likely continue to improve overnight. 

Snow rolls in tomorrow, turning heavier by late afternoon with a mix of snow, sleet, and rain as temps climb into the mid-30s. Expect breezy conditions and about 1–2 inches of accumulation before things transition.

Surfaces continue to get better and with a slight warm up tomorrow coupled with another night of grooming, we should be in decent shape. Our grooming team is after the same plan with Upper K.A. as an addition. Other trails that will see a resurface include Gold Rush, Swinger, and 14 other trails. For the full rundown of groomed terrain, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, Knomes Knoll Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all slated to open at 9:00 a.m. to get you where you want to go. 

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

Have a fun Friday! 

-Evan

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-19 06:05:58
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

Get excited for another welcome back to the hill! Mostly cloudy skies will stick around, with highs near 35 degrees at the base and 28 at the peak. A steady south wind keeps things feeling sharp, so come prepared for a slightly chilly ride out there. 

Conditions will be on the firmer side with groomed runs improving after another overnight resurface. You’ll find areas of loose granular in addition to machine groomed hardpack today. Our grooming team was on their usual Thursday plan with our Prospector and Panhandler terrain parks added back into the mix. Fool’s Gold and Forty Niner among others got a groom. For the full rundown of groomed terrain, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

The Golden Express, Summit Express, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all slated to open at 9:00 a.m. to get you where you want to go. 

One last thing: don’t forget you can still grab your Spring Pass to unlock unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to full spring access for just $199. 

See you out back out there! 

-Evan
